The '''Manarai''', also called '''Menarai'''<ref name="Secrets">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''</ref>, was an upscale [[Restaurant/Legends|restaurant]] located in [[Monument Plaza/Legends|Monument Plaza]] in the [[Manarai Mountains]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The restaurant was built into the wall of the mountains' highest peak, [[Umate/Legends|Umate]]. The [[Master Chef]] of this restaurant was [[Tavvar Va'ran]], recognized as the second best [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] [[Chef/Legends|chef]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

==History==
Sometime during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="Date" /> the Manarai was considered Coruscant's most exclusive restaurant. It sat high atop a spire along [[Monument Plaza/Legends|Monument Plaza]]. If prospective guests could afford the astronomical prices, they still had to be approved by the restaurant's management. Reservations had to be booked months on advance. Of course, it was well worth the wait, dinning at the Manarai was described as a surreal experience. The restaurant was structured across multiple tiers, providing every diner with a panoramic view through the [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] walls, allowing them to admire Monument Park and the Coruscant skyline.<ref name="SES" />

The restaurant staff was as cultured and groomed as its clientele. Renowned throughout the galaxy, the [[Chef/Legends|chefs]] were handpicked by scouts dedicated to discovering new and tantalizing cuisines. One such example was Master Chef Tavvar Va'ran, a Kubaz [[Gender/Legends|male]]. Every member of the serving staff underwent screening and were trained by the Imperial Center's most affluent protocol experts. Security was of great importance at the Manarai, ensuring that patrons can enjoy their meals without the intrusion of outside threats or personal vendettas. Patrons who violated the establishment's standards were rarely permitted to return. From sourcing the finest ingredients from across the Empire and beyond to employing specialized [[Harvester/Legends|harvester]] and hunters for exotic fare, the Manarai spared no expense in catering to the most discerning culinary tastes. Signature dishes included:<ref name="SES" />
*[[Fleek eel/Legends|Fleek eel]] broiled in [[Zaffa oil/Legends|zaffa oil]]
*Stuffed [[Yam/Legends|yams]]
*[[Plicto steak]]
*[[Giant Ithorian snail]] in [[flounut]] butter
*[[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] [[land shrimp]]
*[[Wroonian flycatcher filet]]
*[[Sufar greens]] topped with [[mecolar briddlings]] and [[drezzle sauce]]
*[[Moonglow]]<ref name="Secrets" />

The only confirmed co-owners were [[Dark Prince|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] and [[Guri]]. It was speculated a [[Unidentified Hutt clan lord|Hutt clan lord]], a [[Unidentified coalition of Core World Moffs|coalition of Core World Moffs]], and an [[Unidentified Imperial Advisor (Manarai)|Imperial Advisor]] shared stake in the Manarai. The management ensured that the restaurant itself remained the focus of publicity.<ref name="SES" /> Being the co-owner, Xizor was allowed in any time he wished and had a specific table reserved.<ref name="Secrets" /> Anyone eating at, or who had reservations at that table at the times that he visited were forced to automatically give up the table.
